SEARCH
Module com.examplez.demo
Package com.examplez.demo.Model

Class Game

java.lang.Object
com.examplez.demo.Model.Game
public class Game extends Object
Central model for the turn-based card game.

The class creates the card set, deals hands, tracks the accumulated score, validates human and machine moves, manages the draw and discard piles, and removes players who can no longer make a valid play.

The human turn is coordinated with the controller through the synchronized waitUntilRoundEnds() and endRound() methods.

See Also:
  • Card
  • Player
  • Desk
  • DiscardPile

    Description
    Game(int numberOfPlayers)
    Creates a game configured for the requested number of participants.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addDeskCardToPlayerHand(int turnPlayer)
    Draws one card from the deck and adds it to the player identified by the supplied turn value.
    void
    eliminatePlayer(int turnPlayer)
    Removes a player from the active match and returns that player's hand to the draw deck.
    void
    endRound()
    Marks the human turn as complete and wakes threads waiting for it.
    int
    getAceValueForMachine()
    Chooses the highest valid value for an ace played by a machine.
    int

    Returns the score accumulated by all played cards.

    Returns the cards held by the human player.

    Returns the human player assigned to turn zero.

    Returns the most recently played card.

    Calculates the ace values that remain valid for the current score.

    Checks whether the human player has at least one valid card.

    isMachinePlayerAbleToPlay(int turnPlayer)

    isPlayerHumanCardValid(Card cardPlayed)
    Determines whether a card selected by the human player can be added without exceeding the maximum score.
    void
    processCardPlayedByHumanPlayer(String id, int aceValue)
    Processes a card selected by the human player, removes it from the hand, and draws a replacement card.
    void
    processCardPlayedByMachinePlayer(int turnMachinePlayer)
    Selects and processes a valid card for a machine player.
    void
    restartDesk()
    Recycles eligible discarded cards back into the draw deck.
    void
    startGame()
    Creates the complete card set, chooses the initial discard, deals the player hands, and initializes the draw deck.
    void
    waitUntilRoundEnds()
    Suspends the calling thread while the human player's turn remains active.

  • Constructor Details

    • Game

      public Game(int numberOfPlayers)
      Creates a game configured for the requested number of participants.
      Parameters:
      numberOfPlayers - total number of players in the match

  • Method Details

    • startGame

      public void startGame()
      Creates the complete card set, chooses the initial discard, deals the player hands, and initializes the draw deck.

    • isPlayerHumanCardValid

      public boolean isPlayerHumanCardValid(Card cardPlayed)
      Determines whether a card selected by the human player can be added without exceeding the maximum score.
      Parameters:
      cardPlayed - card selected by the human player
      Returns:
      true when the card can be played under the current score

    • getPossibleAceValues

      public List<Integer> getPossibleAceValues()
      Calculates the ace values that remain valid for the current score.
      Returns:
      list containing 1, 10, or both valid values

    • isMachinePlayerAbleToPlay

      public boolean isMachinePlayerAbleToPlay(int turnPlayer)
      Checks whether a specified machine player has at least one valid card.
      Parameters:
      turnPlayer - turn identifier of the machine player
      Returns:
      true when the machine can make a valid play

    • isHumanPlayerAbleToPlay

      public boolean isHumanPlayerAbleToPlay()
      Checks whether the human player has at least one valid card.
      Returns:
      true when the human can make a valid play

    • processCardPlayedByMachinePlayer

      public void processCardPlayedByMachinePlayer(int turnMachinePlayer)
      Selects and processes a valid card for a machine player.
      Parameters:
      turnMachinePlayer - turn identifier of the machine player

    • processCardPlayedByHumanPlayer

      public void processCardPlayedByHumanPlayer(String id, int aceValue)
      Processes a card selected by the human player, removes it from the hand, and draws a replacement card.
      Parameters:
      id - identifier of the selected card
      aceValue - effective value chosen for the card when it is an ace

    • addDeskCardToPlayerHand

      public void addDeskCardToPlayerHand(int turnPlayer)
      Draws one card from the deck and adds it to the player identified by the supplied turn value. The deck is restarted when it becomes empty.
      Parameters:
      turnPlayer - turn identifier of the player receiving the card

    • getHumanPlayer

      public PlayerHuman getHumanPlayer()
      Returns the human player assigned to turn zero.
      Returns:
      human player, or null when it is no longer active

    • getLastCardPlayed

      public Card getLastCardPlayed()
      Returns the most recently played card.
      Returns:
      active card at the top of the discard pile

    • getHandHumanPlayer

      public List<Card> getHandHumanPlayer()
      Returns the cards held by the human player.
      Returns:
      human hand, or an empty immutable list if no human player remains

    • getTurnPlayers

      public List<Integer> getTurnPlayers()
      Builds the current sequence of active turn identifiers.
      Returns:
      list of turns for all players still in the match

    • restartDesk

      public void restartDesk()
      Recycles eligible discarded cards back into the draw deck.

    • eliminatePlayer

      public void eliminatePlayer(int turnPlayer)
      Removes a player from the active match and returns that player's hand to the draw deck.
      Parameters:
      turnPlayer - turn identifier of the player to eliminate

    • waitUntilRoundEnds

      public void waitUntilRoundEnds() throws InterruptedException
      Suspends the calling thread while the human player's turn remains active.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the waiting thread is interrupted

    • endRound

      public void endRound()
      Marks the human turn as complete and wakes threads waiting for it.

    • getAceValueForMachine

      public int getAceValueForMachine()
      Chooses the highest valid value for an ace played by a machine.
      Returns:
      10 when allowed; otherwise 1

    • getCurrentSumGame

      public int getCurrentSumGame()
      Returns the score accumulated by all played cards.
      Returns:
      current game sum
